what_it_does: Streams main-session JSONL transcripts from ~/.claude/projects/<project>/<session>.jsonl, filters conversation content according to an explicit keep/strip/redact contract, resolves a title fallback chain, rolls up subagent sidechains, and writes one markdown file per session to ~/corpora/claude-sessions/<project>/<session>.md while tracking export state for idempotency.
observations: Implements a binding FILTER LAW with explicit keep/strip rules for line and block types.
Secret redaction uses ordered regexes for private keys, API keys, JWTs, Slack/AWS/GitHub/HuggingFace/Google tokens, plus key=value assignment redaction.
Streams JSONL line-by-line to avoid loading large sessions into memory.
Tool calls are reduced to one-line traces; tool_result bodies and duplicated toolUseResult fields are stripped.
Subagent sidechains are rolled up into final-text sections, skipping files below a 2KB stub threshold.
Title resolution follows ai-title → custom-title → last-prompt → leaf-resolved prompt → first user turn → untitled.
Export is idempotent by comparing markdown content while ignoring the per-run exported_at line.
State file .exporter_state.json records size/mtime to skip unchanged sessions.
Quiesce window (default 15 minutes) prevents re-export of live sessions.
Counters report kept turns, stripped blocks, redaction kinds, unknown line/block types, and unparseable lines.
standouts: The FILTER LAW is the central contract: explicit classification and accounting of every line/block type.
Belt-and-braces secret redaction with ordered regexes plus assignment-value redaction.
Subagent rollup with size threshold and final-text-only summarization.
Stable idempotent write that ignores the exported_at timestamp.
conventions: Fail-loud batch processing: collect per-file errors, print summary, exit 1 on failures.
Atomic temp-file write followed by os.replace.
Derived projection, not a versioned source of truth.
Top-of-file docstring documents laws, run instructions, and title fallback chain.
concerns: Broad except Exception in export_one swallows detailed tracebacks for failed files.
Secret regexes may false-positive or miss novel secret formats; they are not centrally validated.
_stable strips any line starting with exported_at:, which could affect idempotence if user content contains that prefix.
collect_subagents reads entire sidechain files even though only first prompt and final text are kept.
build_agent_index links sidechains by reading only the first line, so missing sessionId leaves them unlinked.
is_noise_user_text treats whitespace-only user text as noise without counting it separately.
notes: A self-contained, policy-driven exporter that turns Claude Code session JSONL into a filtered, redacted, vault-ready markdown corpus while staying read-only on the source catalog.
questions: What is the exact schema of the Claude Code JSONL line and block types referenced here?
Where is the Session Fabric Implementation Guide §E maintained and versioned?
How are the redaction regexes tested and kept current against new secret formats?
How does the downstream vault register and index the generated corpus?
Are subagent file paths and the leafUuid resolution stable across Claude Code versions?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it exports filtered, redacted, idempotent markdown projections of Claude Code sessions.
intention_signals: Create a searchable, regenerable, secret-free memory of agent sessions that downstream systems can treat as a normal markdown vault, without ever mutating the original JSONL catalog.
novelty: Combines a named FILTER LAW with detailed residue accounting, layered regex secret redaction, subagent rollup, and stable-idempotent markdown generation in a single stdlib-only script.
contribution: A concrete exporter that enforces a documented filtering and redaction policy, producing vault-ready markdown from Claude Code session JSONL while reporting what was kept, stripped, redacted, and unknown.
summary_for_embedding: A stdlib-only Python exporter that transforms Claude Code session JSONL transcripts into filtered, secret-redacted, idempotent markdown files under a searchable corpus, with subagent rollups and residue accounting.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a regenerable, privacy-safe markdown projection of agent session transcripts for downstream vault indexing without modifying the source catalog.

symbols.does: Runs ordered regex redactions and key=value assignment redaction over a string, updating a counter.
symbols.description: Applies private-key, API-key, JWT, Slack, AWS, GitHub, HuggingFace, and Google key regexes in order, then redacts values in key=assignment patterns, returning sanitized text and incrementing the counter by kind.

symbols.does: Helper closure that replaces a secret assignment match with a redacted placeholder.
symbols.description: Used inside redact to transform ASSIGNMENT_RE matches into [redacted:assignment] while incrementing the counter.

symbols.does: Returns true for user texts that are machinery prefixes or whitespace-only.
symbols.description: Identifies synthetic or system user lines such as system-reminders, Caveat prefixes, command markers, interruptions, task notifications, the summarizer prompt, and empty strings so they are stripped.

symbols.does: Builds a one-line tool trace from a tool name and a selected input key.
symbols.description: Picks a meaningful scalar argument from TRACE_KEYS or any scalar input, truncates it, and returns Name(arg) so that tool bodies never enter the output.

symbols.does: Converts an ISO timestamp to a human-readable date/time string.
symbols.description: Parses ISO 8601 timestamps with Z handling and formats them as YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM; falls back to the first 16 characters on parse failure.

symbols.does: Stateful parser for a single main-session JSONL stream.
symbols.description: Accumulates filtered conversation turns, session metadata, title candidates, and accounting counters while streaming one session file line by line.

symbols.does: Initializes per-session parser state and counters.
symbols.description: Sets up the turn list, keep/strip counters, redaction counter, unknown-type counters, metadata fields, and the thinking-block inclusion flag.

symbols.does: Dispatches one JSONL line to the appropriate handler.
symbols.description: Parses a raw line, routes user and assistant body types to conversation handling, extracts title metadata, and counts stripped or unknown line types.

symbols.does: Captures session-level metadata from a parsed line.
symbols.description: Records session id, cwd, git branch, version, and first/last timestamps from any line that carries them.

symbols.does: Filters and stores one user or assistant conversation line.
symbols.description: For users, keeps non-noise text blocks and redacts them; for assistants, keeps text and optional thinking blocks, emits one-line tool traces, strips tool_result and image blocks, and counts unknown content-block types.

symbols.does: Appends a part to the current assistant turn, creating one if needed.
symbols.description: Groups consecutive assistant outputs into a single turn so the rendered markdown does not fragment one assistant response across multiple turns.

symbols.does: Chooses a session title using the documented fallback chain.
symbols.description: Returns the best available title and its source label, preferring ai-title, then custom-title, last-prompt text, leaf-resolved prompt, first real user turn, and finally an untitled placeholder.

symbols.does: Rolls up sidechain subagent sessions into heading/final-text sections.
symbols.description: Scans top-level and nested agent-*.jsonl files matching the session, skips files below SUBAGENT_MIN_BYTES, extracts the first real user prompt and the last assistant text, redacts and truncates the final text, and returns markdown sections.

symbols.does: Renders a SessionExport and subagent sections into a markdown document.
symbols.description: Builds YAML frontmatter with session metadata and accounting fields, then emits numbered Tim/Claude turns followed by subagent sections.

symbols.does: Exports a single main session JSONL to markdown.
symbols.description: Parses the file, collects subagents, renders markdown, and writes the output only when the stable content differs from any existing file.

symbols.does: Strips the exported_at line for idempotent comparison.
symbols.description: Local helper inside export_one that returns markdown text with any line starting with exported_at: removed so per-run timestamp changes do not force rewrites.

symbols.does: Maps parent session ids to top-level agent sidechain files.
symbols.description: Reads the first line of each agent-*.jsonl in a project directory and links sidechains by their sessionId field.

symbols.does: Lists main-session JSONL files matching optional filters.
symbols.description: Walks project directories under the source catalog, excludes agent-*.jsonl sidechain files, and returns candidate paths filtered by project and session id.

symbols.does: Loads the exporter state JSON or returns an empty dict.
symbols.description: Reads .exporter_state.json if present and valid; prints a warning and starts fresh when the file is missing or unreadable.

symbols.does: Atomically writes the exporter state JSON.
symbols.description: Writes state to a temporary file and replaces the target, recording per-source size, mtime, and exported_at.

symbols.does: CLI entry point that orchestrates catalog scan, filtering, export, and reporting.
symbols.description: Parses arguments, applies quiesce and unchanged checks, exports sessions, updates state, prints accounting of kept/stripped/redacted/unknown types and errors, and exits non-zero when any file fails.
